Name: CEE
LongName: CEE-ENEV Checklist for Editors Assessing Validity of Systematic Reviews in the Environmental Sector
Description: A practical editor/reviewer checklist from the Collaboration for Environmental Evidence (CEE) and Environmental Evidence for quickly assessing whether manuscripts claiming to be systematic reviews meet core conduct and reporting expectations.
Aim: To support editors and peer reviewers in identifying manuscripts that do or do not qualify as systematic reviews in the environmental sector, based on transparent and replicable methods.

Scope.Coverage: Reviews in the environmental sector that claim systematic-review status and need rapid validity checks for core conduct and reporting requirements.
Scope.Focus: Protocol availability, full methodological transparency, reproducible searching and screening, explicit critical appraisal, transparent extraction and synthesis, and clear discussion of review and evidence-base limitations.
Scope.ContrastWith: This is a discipline-specific editorial validity checklist for environmental systematic reviews. It complements broad systematic-review reporting guidance by providing rapid screening criteria for environmental evidence journals and reviewers.

primary.description: Collaboration for Environmental Evidence and Environmental Evidence. A checklist for Editors for assessing validity of Systematic Reviews in the Environmental sector.

items.item_question: Have the authors pre-registered and/or published a protocol with sufficient methodological detail?
items.item_question_background: An a-priori protocol reduces post-hoc methodological decisions that can increase bias.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract protocol citation/link, access location, and whether it contains methodological detail for review stages.

source_page_refs.quote: Have the authors pre-registered and/or published a protocol for the review?

items.item_question: Does the review include a defined methods section covering all key review stages in replicable detail?
items.item_question_background: Transparent methods are required for reproducibility and validity assessment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether methods describe review question, search, screening, appraisal, extraction, and synthesis stages in replicable detail.

source_page_refs.quote: Does the review include a defined methods section providing a description of all the review stages conducted...

items.item_question: Are all search terms/strings and operators reported so the exact search is replicable?
items.item_question_background: Incomplete search reporting prevents verification and reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract full search strings, Boolean operators, wildcards, and source-specific queries.

source_page_refs.quote: Are all search terms and search strings, with Boolean operators... clearly stated... so that the exact search is replicable...

items.item_question: Are study eligibility criteria precisely defined and explicitly linked to question elements?
items.item_question_background: Ambiguous eligibility criteria can introduce subjectivity and selection bias.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract eligibility criteria details, including question-element mapping and additional limits (design/language/date where applicable).

source_page_refs.quote: Are study eligibility criteria precisely defined so as to be replicable... and expressly related to each key element of the question?

items.item_question: Are record counts reported for search retrieval and exclusions at each screening stage?
items.item_question_background: Screening-flow transparency is essential for auditability of inclusion decisions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract counts at each screening stage and any flow diagram or equivalent record trail.

source_page_refs.quote: Is the number of records ... excluded at each stage of the screening process provided?

items.item_question: Is effort made to identify all relevant sources of bias for each included study (ideally using a recognized tool)?
items.item_question_background: Without study-level appraisal, synthesis findings may be driven by unreliable primary studies.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract appraisal framework/tool, criteria, and evidence that each study was appraised.

source_page_refs.quote: Is an effort made to identify all sources of bias ... relevant to each of the individual studies?

items.item_question: Are all extracted data reported so synthesis can be replicated?
items.item_question_background: Replicable synthesis requires visibility of extracted outcome data and metadata.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether raw outcome metrics and study metadata are reported in tables/spreadsheets.

source_page_refs.quote: Are all extracted data reported in a table or spreadsheet so that the synthesis can be replicated?

items.item_question: Is the synthesis method described in replicable detail and justified against characteristics of included studies?
items.item_question_background: Choice and justification of synthesis method strongly influence review conclusions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract synthesis type, procedural detail, and justification linked to heterogeneity/study characteristics.

source_page_refs.quote: Is the choice of synthesis method ... described in sufficient detail to be replicable? ... justified on the basis of characteristics of included studies...

items.item_question: Is there an explicit discussion of risk of bias in the synthesized evidence due to review-process and primary-study limitations?
items.item_question_background: Explicit limitations reporting is critical for responsible interpretation and use.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract text discussing limitations from both review conduct and primary evidence quality perspectives.

source_page_refs.quote: Is an explicit section ... devoted to the authors’ consideration of risk of bias in the synthesized evidence base...

EvidenceExamplesPositive: This environmental evidence synthesis provides protocol registration, full replicable search strings, explicit eligibility criteria, and transparent screening counts.
The review reports study-level critical appraisal and provides extracted data tables enabling replication of synthesis.
Authors justify the synthesis approach and explicitly discuss limitations and risk of bias in the evidence base.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: The manuscript is a narrative environmental review with no protocol, no replicable search strings, and no transparent screening workflow.
This paper labels itself a systematic review but omits critical appraisal and extracted data needed for synthesis replication.
A biomedical SR paper with no environmental evidence context is out of scope for this checklist.
Matching.Explain: Adds a discipline-specific environmental systematic-review validity checklist for editorial/reviewer triage of manuscripts that claim SR status in environmental evidence contexts.
